Name: assembler
Description: The underlying tool to use for the assembly module. Note: 'spades' support is currently under active development and not yet available.

Name: megahit-min-len
Description: The minimum contig length cutoff for the MEGAHIT assembly.

Name: megahit-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to MEGAHIT.

Name: spades-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to SPAdes.

Name: spades-memory
Description: The maximum memory (in GB) allocated to SPAdes for assembly.

Name: dwnld-params
Description: Additional parameters for downloading raw FASTQ files from the NCBI database using entrez-fetch.

Name: dwnld-only
Description: If TRUE,  only remote SRA files will be downloaded from the NCBI database without executing downstream preprocessing steps.

Name: pigz-params
Description: Additional parameters passed to pigz for parallel gzip compression of FASTQ files downloaded from the SRA.

Name: fastp-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to fastp during preprocessing.

Name: hostile-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to Hostile during decontamination.

Name: hostile-index-name
Description: The name of the pre-built Hostile index to use. Ensure the index exists in the local Hostile installation (see https://github.com/bede/hostile for details).

Name: decontam-host
Description: If TRUE, host decontamination will be performed using Hostile after initial adapter and quality trimming with fastp. The host index can be specified via --hostile-index-name or the config['hostile-index-name'] key.

Name: hostile-aligner
Description: The short-read aligner to use for host decontamination ('bowtie2' or 'minimap2'). Note: Hostile does not currently recommend using minimap2 for short reads.

Name: hostile-aligner-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass to the underlying Hostile aligner.

Name: NCBI-email
Description: The email address associated with NCBI E-utilities requests for data download and verification.

Name: NCBI-API-key
Description: The NCBI API key used to authorize higher request rates when fetching large volumes of data (see https://ncbiinsights.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/2017/11/02/new-api-keys-for-the-e-utilities).

Name: humann-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to HUMAnN.

Name: binning-consensus
Description: If TRUE, executes a consensus binning workflow using MetaBAT2, MaxBin2, and CONCOCT, followed by consensus pooling via DAS Tool. If FALSE, a GPU-accelerated VAMB approach will be used instead.

Name: strobealign-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to strobealign.

Name: MetaBAT2-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to MetaBAT2.

Name: MaxBin2-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to MaxBin2.

Name: CONCOCT-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to CONCOCT.

Name: jgi-summarize-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass to the jgi_summarize_bam_contig_depths command.

Name: DASTool-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to DAS Tool.

Name: checkm2-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to CheckM2.

Name: galah-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to galah.

Name: GTDBTk-identify-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass to the 'gtdbtk identify' command.

Name: GTDBTk-align-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass to the 'gtdbtk align' command.

Name: GTDBTk-classify-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass to the 'gtdbtk classify' command.

Name: VAMB-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to VAMB.

Name: mpa-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to MetaPhlAn.

Name: mpa-indexv
Description: The index version to use for MetaPhlAn. If the specified index is not found locally,  it will be downloaded automatically.

Name: hostile-index-db
Description: The directory path where the Hostile database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to 'workflow/databases/' relative to the Snakemake base directory.

Name: PhaBox2-db
Description: The directory path where the PhaBox2 database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to 'workflow/databases/' relative to the Snakemake base directory.

Name: genomad-db
Description: The directory path where the geNomad database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to 'workflow/databases/' relative to the Snakemake base directory.

Name: virsorter2-db
Description: The directory path where the VirSorter2 database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to 'workflow/databases/' relative to the Snakemake base directory.

Name: vibrant-db
Description: The directory path where the VIBRANT database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to 'workflow/databases/' relative to the Snakemake base directory.

Name: checkv-db
Description: The directory path where the CheckV database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to 'workflow/databases/' relative to the Snakemake base directory.

Name: iphop-db
Description: The directory path where the iPHoP database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to 'workflow/databases/' relative to the Snakemake base directory.

Name: iphop-db-basename
Description: The base name of the iPHoP database. Ensure compatibility between the base name and the version as outlined in the documentation (https://bitbucket.org/srouxjgi/iphop/src/main/#markdown-header-host-databases-and-versions).

Name: eggNOG-db
Description: The directory path where the eggNOG database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to 'workflow/databases/' relative to the Snakemake base directory.

Name: eggNOG-db-params
Description: Command-line options and parameters used when downloading the eggNOG database (see https://github.com/eggnogdb/eggnog-mapper for configuration details).

Name: humann-db
Description: The directory path where the HUMAnN3 database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to 'workflow/databases/' relative to the Snakemake base directory.

Name: checkm2-db
Description: The directory path where the CheckM2 database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to 'workflow/databases/' relative to the Snakemake base directory.

Name: GTDBTk-db
Description: The directory path where the GTDB-Tk database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to 'workflow/databases/' relative to the Snakemake base directory.

Name: GTDBTk-db-version
Description: The reference data version of the GTDB-Tk database to use. Ensure the database version matches the installed GTDB-Tk software version (see https://ecogenomics.github.io/GTDBTk/installing/index.html#gtdb-tk-reference-data).

Name: dram-db
Description: The directory path where the DRAM database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to 'workflow/databases/' relative to the Snakemake base directory.

Name: dram-setup-params
Description: Additional parameters to pass to the DRAM setup command during database initialization.

Name: metacerberus-db
Description: The directory path where the MetaCerberus database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to 'workflow/databases/' relative to the Snakemake base directory.

Name: metacerberus-setup-params
Description: Additional parameters to pass to the MetaCerberus 'metacerberus-setup.py' command during database initialization.

Name: pharokka-db
Description: The directory path where the Pharokka database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to 'workflow/databases/' relative to the Snakemake base directory.

Name: phabox2-db-name
Description: The specific package name used to download the database in the format '{phabox-db-baselink}/{phabox-db-name}.zip'.

Name: phabox2-db-baselink
Description: The base remote URL or release link used to fetch the PhaBox2 database archives.

Name: iphop-db-version
Description: The version string of the iPHoP database. Ensure compatibility with the software version as described in the iPHoP database repository (https://bitbucket.org/srouxjgi/iphop/src/main/#markdown-header-host-databases-and-versions).

Name: eggNOG-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to the eggNOG-mapper.

Name: PhaVIP-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to PhaVIP.

Name: virsorter2-annotate-params
Description: Additional parameters to pass to the 'virsorter run' command during the viral annotation phase preceding DRAM-v.

Name: dram-v-annotate-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass to the 'DRAM-v.py annotate' command.

Name: dram-v-distill-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass to the 'DRAM-v.py distill' command.

Name: metacerberus-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to the MetaCerberus 'metacerberus.py' entrypoint.

Name: pharokka-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to Pharokka.

Name: dvf-min-len
Description: The minimum contig length required for DeepVirFinder analysis. All contigs below this threshold will be filtered out.

Name: phamer-min-len
Description: The minimum contig length required for PhaMer analysis. All contigs below this threshold will be filtered out.

Name: dvf-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to DeepVirFinder.

Name: phamer-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to PhaMer.

Name: virsorter2-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to VirSorter2.

Name: vf-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to VirFinder.

Name: vibrant-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to VIBRANT.

Name: seeker-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to Seeker.

Name: ppr-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to PPR-META.

Name: dvf-cutoff
Description: The minimum score threshold for DeepVirFinder to classify a contig as viral.

Name: dvf-pval
Description: The maximum p-value threshold for DeepVirFinder to classify a contig as viral.

Name: phamer-pred
Description: The focus category for which PhaMer will evaluate and identify target viral sequences.

Name: phamer-cutoff
Description: The minimum score threshold for PhaMer prediction to classify a contig as viral.

Name: vf-cutoff
Description: The minimum score threshold for VirFinder to classify a contig as viral.

Name: virsorter2-cutoff
Description: The minimum score threshold for VirSorter2 to classify a contig as viral.

Name: seeker-cutoff
Description: The minimum score threshold for Seeker to classify a contig as viral.

Name: ppr-cutoff
Description: The minimum score threshold for PPR-META to classify a contig as viral.

Name: vibrant-cutoff
Description: The minimum score threshold for VIBRANT to classify a contig as viral.

Name: coverm-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to CoverM.

Name: coverm-methods
Description: The coverage and abundance calculation methods outputted by CoverM. Note: This parameter is primary used for debugging and should not be modified under normal operations.

Name: iphop-host
Description: If TRUE,  executes host prediction using iPHoP instead of CHERRY. Warning: iPHoP requires a very large reference database and has substantial memory overhead; CHERRY is recommended for standard runs.

Name: contig-min-len
Description: The global minimum length threshold for evaluating sequences in the viral-identify module. Shorter contigs will be ignored.

Name: genomad-min-len
Description: The minimum contig length required for geNomad analysis. All contigs below this threshold will be filtered out.

Name: genomad-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass to geNomad during the viral identification workflow.

Name: genomad-cutoff
Description: The core score cutoff threshold used by geNomad to identify a contig as viral.

Name: genomad-cutoff-s
Description: The secondary filtering threshold used by geNomad to identify viral sequences. Setting this value to 0 disables the secondary filter (recommended).

Name: checkv-original
Description: If TRUE,  forces the execution of the standard CheckV tool instead of the faster and more memory-efficient CheckV-PyHMMER implementation (not recommended).

Name: checkv-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to CheckV.

Name: clustering-fast
Description: If TRUE, uses a highly optimized, MEGABlast-based clustering approach to expedite viral Operational Taxonomic Unit (vOTU) generation.

Name: cdhit-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to CD-HIT.

Name: vOTU-ani
Description: The average nucleotide identity (ANI) percentage threshold for fast clustering. Set to 95% by default in strict accordance with MIUViG guidelines (see https://doi.org/10.1038/nbt.4306).

Name: vOTU-targetcov
Description: The minimum target coverage percentage alignment threshold for fast clustering. Set to 85% by default following MIUViG guidelines (see https://doi.org/10.1038/nbt.4306).

Name: vOTU-querycov
Description: The alignment coverage percentage threshold for query sequences when utilizing the fast clustering method.

Name: phagcn-min-len
Description: The minimum contig length required for PhaGCN taxonomic assignment. Shorter contigs are automatically excluded.

Name: phagcn-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to PhaGCN.

Name: genomad-params-tax
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass to geNomad within the viral taxonomy module.

Name: iphop-cutoff
Description: The confidence or score cutoff percentage required by iPHoP to assign a viral sequence to a specific host taxon.

Name: iphop-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to iPHoP.

Name: CHERRY-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to CHERRY.

Name: PhaTYP-params
Description: Additional command-line parameters to pass directly to PhaTYP.

Name: module
Description: The specific functional component or workflow step of vOMIX-MEGA to execute.

Name: workdir
Description: The root working directory for the Snakemake execution environment. Avoid modifying this parameter unless performing advanced adjustments or debugging workflows.

Name: basedir
Description: The directory containing the primary Snakefile configuration. Avoid modifying this parameter unless performing advanced configuration updates or pipeline debugging.

Name: outdir
Description: The path to the output directory where structured vOMIX-MEGA results will be stored. New paths will automatically create parent/nested directories; existing directories will have files appended or overwritten.

Name: datadir
Description: The directory housing raw FASTQ files. This directory is checked to verify if data has been pre-downloaded or is targeted as the output destination for new sequence fetches.

Name: samplelist
Description: The path to the sample metadata list CSV file ('sample_list.csv') used to initialize samples and map their explicit paths (refer to the project Wiki at https://github.com/holab-hku/vOMIX-MEGA/wiki).

Name: fasta
Description: The path to an individual input FASTA file for modules that accept single-file generation. The target file extension must be '.fasta', '.fa', or '.fna'.

Name: fastadir
Description: The path to a directory containing multiple input FASTA files. The pipeline will automatically select all valid filenames ending with '.fasta', '.fa', or '.fna'.

Name: sample-name
Description: An explicit string token designating the output sample profile name when using individual file modes via the '--fasta' flag or 'config[\fasta\"]'."

Name: assembly-ids
Description: A structured array list specifying the sample identifiers mapping sequentially to files located inside your fastadir path (e.g. ['sampleA', 'SampleB']). Note: This feature is under evaluation; use with caution.

Name: latest-run
Description: An internal automated timestamp tracking parameter utilized for transaction logging and run history maintenance within the hidden local '.vomix' subfolder.

Name: splits
Description: The number of parallel batches (N) to divide large datasets into when running tools like geNomad to optimize memory distribution. A value of 0 processes the data as a single block.

Name: keep-intermediates
Description: If TRUE, retains all heavy structural intermediate files created during downstream processes, such as raw fastp outputs prior to host decontamination routines.

Name: setup-database
Description: Determines if database dependencies should be validated or retrieved when initializing non-setup modules. Snakemake tracks existing builds to prevent redundancy unless overridden via '--forcerun' (-F).

Name: max-cores
Description: The maximum ceiling of processing cores allocated to separate parallel Snakemake rules. Note: This parameter manages pipeline distribution boundaries and operates independently of standard thread flags (-j/-n).
